evals.name: go-log-exporter
evals.prompt: Enable JSON logging for qubership-log-exporter — config and deployment defaults only; do not rewrite log call sites.
evals.expected_output: Single Go service: JSON formatter wired, Helm/chart exposes format selection, smoke JSON line documented.
expectations: The agent scans the repository root for runtime markers (go.mod, pom.xml, charts) before editing.
The agent identifies the Go binary and Helm chart as the deployable component before editing.
The agent limits production code edits to logger bootstrap and config — no bulk call-site refactors.
Helm or deployment config exposes JSON format selection (LOG_FORMAT, LME_LOG_FORMAT, or documented equivalent).
The agent uses repo-documented build and smoke commands and records doc reference plus exact command in the report.
The agent captures or documents one app log line with time, level, and message fields.
The report uses stage enable, records run-start HEAD, and includes a gates table.
The agent documents blocked validation with concrete error and missing prerequisite when local validation fails.
Objective check: check_enable_gates.py passes on the worktree (or blocked items are documented in the report).

evals.name: java-dbaas-monorepo
evals.prompt: Enable JSON logging for qubership-dbaas — both dbaas-aggregator and dbaas-operator; config/Helm only, no call-site migration.
evals.expected_output: Monorepo: both aggregator and operator in ledger; JSON config and Helm per component; smoke or blocked per component.
expectations: The agent scans the repository root for runtime markers such as go.mod, pom.xml, and helm-templates before editing.
The agent lists both dbaas-aggregator and dbaas-operator in the component ledger before claiming done.
The agent produces a component ledger with stack, LOG_FORMAT/encoder status, and enabled/blocked/pending per component.
The agent wires Quarkus/Logback JSON encoder config and Helm for the Java aggregator, or marks Java blocked with Maven/auth evidence.
The agent wires Go JSON format path and Helm LOG_FORMAT for dbaas-operator.
The agent does not mark the monorepo fully enabled while a buildable component was skipped without a blocked reason.
The agent follows repo docs for operator local path (e.g. dev/kind) or bootstrap/CI when smoking logs.
The agent documents smoke JSON evidence per enabled component (not unit tests alone).
The report uses stage enable, records run-start HEAD, and includes a gates table.
Objective check: check_enable_gates.py passes on the worktree (partial blocked for Java is acceptable when documented).
